blocks.value: Uploads a Concert-native vm_scan CSV (CVE, Host IPAddress, Package, Package Version, Package Path, severity, Score, hasFix, Fixed Version, Description, Host Name - see IBM/Concert's use-case-data-files/vulnerabilities/vm_scan/vm_scan_concert_sample.csv), built from a Trivy JSON scan. Switched away from uploading raw CycloneDX/JSON with data_type=vm_scan after confirming live that Concert's vm_scan ingestion is format-specific per scanner_name (aws_inspector/nessus/qualys/concert) and doesn't attribute CVEs to a distinct host resource for an unrecognized scanner_name like 'trivy' - it still 202s and creates flat CVE Findings under the application, but Arena view can't differentiate them from application-code CVEs.

inputs.name: command
inputs.value:
"for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  sudo trivy rootfs --scanners vuln --format json --quiet --output /tmp/trivy-scan.json / && break
  sleep 3
done
cat /tmp/trivy-scan.json
sudo rm -f /tmp/trivy-scan.json"

blocks.name: BuildVmScanCsv

blocks.action: system/IBM/Concert v2/Import Data/Upload Files to Concert
blocks.name: IngestScan

meta.description: Common/SSH credential stored in Concert for the target host.
Stored Concert API credential (protocol/host/api key/instance id).
IP address or hostname of the scanned host - populates the Host IPAddress/Host Name columns so Concert attributes these CVEs to this specific host.

Scans a remote Linux host over SSH for OS-level vulnerabilities using Trivy, converts the result into Concert's native vm_scan CSV format (scanner_name=concert), and ingests it via the official Import Data/Upload Files to Concert block so CVEs attribute to the host as a distinct resource in Concert's Arena view.
